Adolescent Family Life Program
Purpose
This program is designed to address and enhance the health, social, economic, and educational well-being of expectant and parenting adolescents/youth and their children in California. The program supports youth in building resilience, strengths, and skills to thrive.
Goals
Increase social and emotional support
Improve health birth outcomes
Improve pregnancy planning and spacing
Increase educational attainment- School graduation and educational or vocational advancement
Increase access to and utilization of needed services
Services
Social workers provide comprehensive case management services on a home visitation model to eligible expectant and/or parenting teens/youth. They meet with clients 2x a month. Social workers work predominantly with the low-income, high-risk teen population. Provides education and 12-24 months of free case management services. Our Social workers assess the client’s situation and resource needs while prioritizing personal goals. They assist with staying or returning to school and attaining a positive outcome. This program helps develop effective parenting skills and obtain social/ economic independence by building self -esteem and self-worth.
Who Is Eligible?
Eligible youth must be:
Expecting and/or Parenting female and male who is 21 years old or younger
Resides in Ventura County

The official text is the English version of the website. Any discrepancies or differences created in the translation are not binding and have no legal effect for compliance or enforcement purposes.
